summary:
Estrogen is not an effective treatment for postpartum hemorrhage because it does not act as a uterotonic agent and has no proven benefit in addressing uterine atony. All other listed options—such as oxytocin, prostaglandins, and synthetic combinations—are well-established interventions supported by clinical guidelines.

The summary above contains more than two sentences. Please revise strictly within exactly two sentences while preserving only essential clinical facts related directly to treating postpartum hemorrhage.
